page: Amazon Web Services r8gd.medium is a memory-optimized instance powered by a single dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 processor core running at 2.8 GHz. The instance features 8.0 GB of system memory, establishing a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It includes 59 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. The underlying virtualization is managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates average single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1694.58 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1741.0. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression is a notable strength, registering at 14.0 GB/sec. However, due to the single-core configuration, multi-core benchmarks are limited, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 1736.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 1694.58, both falling into the lower performance percentiles. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with large block read-only speeds at 30.06 GB/sec and write speeds at 11.68 GB/sec.

From a resource density perspective, the r8gd.medium offers a cost-efficient entry point for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high parallel processing capabilities. The inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage provides low-latency scratch space, though the single-core limitation restricts its utility for highly threaded applications. This instance is suited for lightweight database operations, caching layers, single-threaded data processing, and basic web serving.

However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain low due to the single-vCPU constraint. This instance represents a cost-efficient option for memory-intensive, single-threaded workloads, making it suitable for lightweight database tasks, caching, and basic web serving.

meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8gd.medium is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance with 1 vCPU, 8.0 GB RAM, and 59 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring a dedicated single-core processor and local NVMe SSD storage for lightweight, memory-intensive workloads.
